compiled_state.title: Enforce plugin boundary: relocate dev-only artifacts and add allowlist pre-commit hook
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Context
DSO plugin developers — and AI agents acting as developers — currently place dev-team-targeted artifacts (ADRs, design docs, research findings, migration guides, dev tests) inside plugins/dso/, causing them to be packaged and shipped into every host project's plugin cache alongside user-facing content. End users receive contributor noise; developers have no enforced boundary distinguishing shipped from local-only content. Without automation, agents writing new docs or tests will continue violating this boundary silently.

## Success Criteria
- All dev-team-targeted files are relocated from plugins/dso/docs/ and plugins/dso/tests/ to their root equivalents (docs/adr/, docs/designs/, docs/findings/, docs/reference/, docs/, .claude/docs/, tests/skills/)
- A pre-commit hook exists with an explicit allowlist of permitted path patterns inside plugins/dso/ and blocks any commit adding a file outside that allowlist
- A CLAUDE.md rule defines the plugin boundary with concrete examples of shipped vs. local-only artifact categories
- The doc-writer agent definition is updated to include docs/designs/, docs/findings/, and docs/archive/ as valid output locations for dev-team artifact types
- A fresh plugin cache install (simulated by inspecting the plugin manifest or published cache) contains no ADRs, migration guides, spike reports, or dev tests
- An agent attempting to create a file matching a dev-only pattern inside plugins/dso/ is blocked at commit time with a message directing to the correct local path

## Dependencies
None

## Approach
Relocate all misplaced files to their root equivalents in a single migration commit, then add an allowlist-based pre-commit hook (matching the pattern of existing hooks in plugins/dso/hooks/dispatchers/) that rejects new files in plugins/dso/ not matching a maintained allowlist of shipped-content paths. Pair with CLAUDE.md and doc-writer agent updates so agents have both a rule and enforcement backing it.

### Planning Intelligence Log
- **Web research (Step 2.6)**: not triggered
  - Bright-line conditions that fired: none
- **Scenario analysis (Step 2.75)**: not triggered
  - Scenarios surviving blue team filter: skipped
- **Practitioner-requested additional cycles**: none
- **Follow-on scrutiny (Step 0)**: not triggered
- **Feasibility resolution (Step 2.5)**: not triggered
- **LLM-instruction signal (Step 5)**: not triggered
